heroHeadline: PLA Sorties Spike to 24 Day After Liaoning Carrier Transit — Highest Non-Exercise Count in 2026; 23 Days to Trump-Xi Summit
heroSubtitle: PLA aircraft sorties surged to 24 on April 21 — the highest non-exercise daily count since Justice Mission-2025 (December 2025) — with 11 crossing the median line into Taiwan's northern, southwestern, and eastern ADIZ simultaneously. The spike follows China's aircraft carrier Liaoning (CV-16) transiting the Taiwan Strait on April 20 with 12 PLAN vessels (highest ship count since the April 11 post-KMT-summit surge), constituting a two-day PLA escalation operation ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing on May 14-15. Xi Jinping has called for 'prudence on US arms sales to Taiwan' in his last phone call with Trump; analysts warn Beijing will seek incremental gains — not a breakthrough — to weaken Taiwan-US security ties at the summit. JMSDF destroyer Ikazuchi transited the Taiwan Strait on April 17 — first under PM Takaichi — prompting China's East China Sea readiness patrols on April 18. Taiwan's NT$1.25T special defense budget remains in legislative negotiations with 23 days to the summit.
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, CNBC, MarineLink, Stars and Stripes, Japan News Pravda, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
